behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff boundary (collectors -> aggregator -> summary input), not inside one pure function. Use in-process fakes/stubs for the 3 source clients and assert the payload sent to the summarizer contains all sections. Rejected System: behavior text is about payload completeness, not real network/OS effects. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no requirement to validate model judgment or multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/orchestration modules with stubbed metrics, issue-tracker, and deployment-log adapters returning distinct fixtures. Run one report build and assert the exact input object passed to summary generation includes all three source datasets with correct section mapping.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter failure -> orchestration -> report composer). In-process failure injection (exception/timeout from one adapter) can reproduce the real logic bug. Rejected System: no concrete behavior text requiring real OS/network semantics beyond an adapter failure signal. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: correctness is fallback control-flow, not model-output quality or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source stubs returning data and one stub raising an availability error. Assert the run completes, the affected section contains `data unavailable`, and other sections are still included.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on quality/correctness of generated narrative content, which is model judgment. Stubbed outputs would hide real hallucination/omission errors. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic decision point (summary generation), not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative weekly data cases to the real summary model and evaluate factual fidelity against key-value expectations (e.g., critical metrics/issues/deploy counts must be present and numerically consistent). Run multiple trials with acceptance thresholds for omission/misstatement rates.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Real external boundaries are the point of failure: HTTP/API collection and actual email transport/inbox receipt. In-process mocks can miss SMTP/API auth, TLS, provider behavior, and delivery-path issues. Rejected Agentic: behavior text targets end-to-end delivery to inbox, not correctness of model judgment; summary content quality is covered by behavior 3.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script in an environment with real internal endpoints (or production-like staging endpoints) and a real mail service/account. Verify one scheduled/manual run produces an email in the configured inbox with expected report structure and source sections.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Failure spans real infrastructure boundaries: email transport outage plus filesystem persistence across runs/schedule boundary. Mocks often hide real retry/persistence timing and file-handling issues. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: requirement is delivery fallback and retry mechanics, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run once with mail endpoint intentionally unreachable and verify report is persisted to local fallback file. Run the next scheduled execution with mail service restored and verify the previously saved report is retried/sent, then fallback state is cleared or marked delivered.
